heading: United States contributions to Pennsylvania: authority, conditions, limitations
label: 572 United States contributions to Pennsylvania: authority, conditions, limitations

children.heading: Contributions to be matched by Commonwealth
children.label: a Contributions to be matched by Commonwealth
children.text: The amounts authorized to be contributed by the Secretary of the Interior to the Commonwealth shall be equally matched by the Commonwealth;

children.heading: Amount of contributions authorized
children.label: b Amount of contributions authorized
children.text: The total amount of contributions by the Secretary of the Interior under the authority of this chapter shall not exceed $8,500,000, of which $1,500,000 of the unexpended balance remaining as of , shall be reserved for the control and drainage of water;July 31, 1962

children.heading: Limitation on use of contribution
children.label: c Limitation on use of contribution
children.text: The amounts contributed by the Secretary of the Interior under the authority of this chapter and the equally matched amounts contributed by the Commonwealth shall not be used for operating and maintaining projects constructed pursuant to this chapter or for the purchase of culm, rock, or spoil banks;

children.heading: Commonwealth responsible for installation and operation of projects
children.label: d Commonwealth responsible for installation and operation of projects
children.text: The Commonwealth shall have full responsibility for installing, operating, and maintaining projects constructed pursuant to this chapter, and shall give evidence, satisfactory to the Secretary of the Interior, that it will enforce effective installation, operation, and maintenance safeguards;

children.heading: Location and operation of projects
children.label: e Location and operation of projects
children.text: Projects constructed pursuant to this chapter shall be so located, operated, and maintained as to provide the maximum conservation of anthracite coal resources or, in those instances where such work would be in the interest of the public health or safety, to seal abandoned coal mines and to fill voids in abandoned coal mines, and, where possible, to avoid creating inequities among those mines which may be affected by the waters to be controlled thereby; and

children.heading: Economic justification for abandoned coal mine projects
children.label: f Economic justification for abandoned coal mine projects
children.text: Projects for the sealing of abandoned coal mines or the filling of voids in abandoned coal mines shall be determined by the Secretary of the Interior to be economically justified. The Secretary shall not find any project to be economically justified unless the potential benefits are estimated by him to exceed the estimated cost of the project.
